onboarding.label: Getting started
onboarding.title: Welcome to Ever Works
onboarding.subtitle: Build and publish your first directory in minutes.

welcome.title: What is Ever Works?
welcome.description: Ever Works is an open-source directory builder. Create structured content directories — tools, jobs, links, products — and publish them as live websites.
welcome.detail: Let's get you set up. Connect your providers below, then create your first directory.

feature1.title: AI-powered content
feature1.description: Generate and enrich directory items automatically using your AI provider.

feature2.title: Instant publishing
feature2.description: Deploy to Vercel with one click — no server configuration needed.

feature3.title: Git-backed
feature3.description: Every directory is a GitHub repository you own and control.

directory.title: Create your first directory
directory.description: A directory is your content hub. Add items, configure the layout, and publish it in one click.
directory.detail: You're all set! Create your first directory to start building and publishing content.

publish.title: Go live
publish.description: Connect Vercel or GitHub to deploy your directory as a public website — no hosting configuration needed.

pluginStep.noSettings: No configuration required for this plugin.
pluginStep.getTokenPrompt: Create a token first, then paste it below and save to verify the connection.

plugins.byokDescription: Connect your own key to unlock the full configuration.
plugins.setupTokenDescription: Create a token first, then paste it below and save to verify the connection.

errors.loadStatus: Failed to load device authentication status.

verify.title: Verify
verify.description: Save settings and verify the connection.
verify.cardDescription: Save the current settings and run the plugin connection validation.

apiKey.title: API Key
apiKey.description: Use a user-scoped API key for this plugin.

deviceAuth.title: Device Authentication
deviceAuth.description: Start a device-code flow for this plugin and complete it in your browser.

status.title: Device Authentication Status
status.fallback: Start or refresh device authentication for this plugin.
status.backendHint: Device authentication is stored in a managed backend workspace for your user account. It must be completed and used on the same Ever Works deployment.

actions.notInstalled: The required CLI is not installed on this machine.

claudeWizard.label: Claude Code setup
claudeWizard.title: Set up Claude Code in three steps
claudeWizard.subtitle: This flow keeps the recommended configuration visible and verifies the connection before you finish.

model.title: Pick a model
model.description: Sonnet is preselected as the recommended balance of quality and speed, but you can switch to any supported model.

credentials.title: Add credentials
credentials.description: Paste your Claude Code OAuth token or an Anthropic API key. Either one works.

verify.title: Verify connection
verify.description: Save settings and run a live connection check against Claude before finishing.
verify.checkDescription: Saving here runs a Claude API or CLI check depending on which credential you supplied.

codexWizard.label: Codex setup
codexWizard.title: Set up Codex in three steps
codexWizard.subtitle: This flow keeps the recommended configuration visible and verifies the connection before you finish.

model.title: Pick a model
model.description: Choose the Codex model you want to run for end-to-end directory generation.

credentials.title: Add credentials
credentials.description: Choose whether Codex should use an OpenAI API key or the Codex CLI login available on the backend machine.

api.title: OpenAI API key
api.description: Use an OpenAI API key for Codex execution.

local.title: Backend Codex login
local.description: Reuse the Codex CLI session already available on the machine running Ever Works.
local.machineDescription: This checks `codex login status` and the local Codex auth file on the backend machine.
local.notInstalled: Codex CLI is not installed on the backend machine.
local.error: Failed to read Codex CLI authentication status.

verify.title: Verify connection
verify.description: Save settings and run a live connection check against Codex before finishing.
verify.checkDescription: Saving here runs a Codex API-key or local-CLI validation depending on how you configured authentication.

geminiWizard.label: Gemini setup
geminiWizard.title: Set up Gemini in three steps
geminiWizard.subtitle: Choose how Gemini should authenticate on this machine, then verify the connection before you finish.

mode.title: Choose auth mode
mode.description: Pick the Gemini authentication flow you want this plugin to use, and confirm the model at the same time.

credentials.title: Configure access
credentials.description: Add the credentials required for the selected Gemini authentication flow.

verify.title: Verify connection
verify.description: Save settings and run a live Gemini validation before finishing.

api-key.description: Paste a Gemini API key from Google AI Studio.
api-key.verifyHint: Saving here verifies your Gemini API key against the selected model.

vertex.description: Provide the Google Cloud project details Gemini should use for Vertex AI.
vertex.verifyHint: Saving here runs a live Gemini CLI validation using your Vertex AI configuration.

simWizard.label: SIM AI setup
simWizard.title: Set up SIM AI in two steps
simWizard.subtitle: Add your SIM AI API key and verify the connection to start delegating directory generation to SIM workflows.

credentials.title: Add API key
credentials.description: Paste your SIM AI API key from your dashboard. Optionally set a custom base URL if you use a self-hosted instance.

verify.title: Verify connection
verify.description: Save your API key and run a live connection check against SIM AI.
verify.checkTitle: Run a live SIM AI validation
verify.checkDescription: Saving here verifies your API key is valid and can connect to SIM AI.

connectGithub.title: Connect your GitHub account
connectGithub.description: GitHub is required for creating and managing directory repositories. Connect your account to get started.

newUser.title: Welcome to {companyName}! 🎉
newUser.description: Your account has been created successfully. Please check your email to verify your account.

emailVerification.description: We've sent a verification link to your email address. Please check your inbox and spam folder.

verified.title: Email verified successfully! ✅
verified.description: Your email address has been verified. You now have full access to all features.

oauthConflict.description: This {provider} account is already linked to another Ever Works user.

header.subtitle: Manage your AI-powered directories and track their performance

help.title: Help & Resources

onboarding.title: Onboarding
onboarding.description: Reopen the guided setup anytime and continue where you left off.

quickTips.title: Quick Tips
quickTips.tip1: Create your first directory by clicking the 'Create Directory' button on the dashboard.
quickTips.tip2: Use the AI generator to automatically populate your directory with relevant items.
quickTips.tip3: Connect your GitHub account to sync and deploy your directories as repositories.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage your account settings and preferences

data.exportDescription: Download your account data including directories, plugin settings, and profile information as a JSON file.
data.includeSecrets: Include secret settings (API keys, tokens) — exported as masked values
data.secretsWarning: Secret settings will be exported as masked values. You will need to manually replace them with real credentials before importing.
data.importDescription: Import data from a previously exported JSON file. You can resolve conflicts for directories with matching slugs.
data.syncDescription: Sync your account configuration to a private GitHub repository for backup and migration between instances.

import.dropzone: Drag and drop a JSON export file here, or click to browse
import.missingPlugins: These plugins are not installed and will be skipped
import.secretsIncluded: This export includes secret settings that will be imported.
import.maskedSecretsWarning: This file contains masked secret values (MASKED:***). Please open the JSON file and replace all masked values with your real API keys and credentials before importing. Masked values will be skipped during import.

sync.connectGitHubDescription: Connect your GitHub account in the Git Providers settings to enable sync.
sync.secretsWarning: Secret values will be stored as masked placeholders in your GitHub repository. Real credentials are never pushed to GitHub.

card.title: Automation

subtitle.disabled: Automate refreshes without subscription limits—set cadence and failure guards freely.

fields.automationHelp: Pause to temporarily stop future runs.

generator.companyInfoDescription: Optional company details for branded content

generator.captureScreenshotsDescription: Automatically capture screenshots or product images for each item during generation
generator.aiFirstGenerationDescription: Enable AI-generation of initial items before web search.

generator.initialCategoriesDescription: Seed categories to organize items. The AI will use these as a starting point.
generator.priorityCategoriesDescription: Categories that will appear first in the generated output. Useful for highlighting important sections.
generator.targetKeywordsDescription: Keywords to guide the AI search and content generation. More specific keywords yield better results.
generator.sourceUrlsFieldDescription: Specific URLs to extract content from. If empty, the generator will search the web automatically.
generator.generationMethodDescription: Choose whether to update existing items or recreate everything from scratch.
generator.websiteRepositoryCreationMethodDescripti: How the website repository is created. Template is recommended for most cases.
generator.maxSearchQueriesDescription: Number of search queries the AI will generate to find relevant content (1-50).
generator.resultsPerQueryDescription: Maximum results to retrieve per search query (1-20).
generator.maxPagesToProcessDescription: Maximum web pages to process for content extraction (1-100).
generator.relevanceThresholdDescription: Minimum relevance score (0-1) for content to be included. Higher values mean stricter filtering.
generator.dataGenerationDescription: Configure data volume and entity generation
generator.generateCategoriesDescription: Create category structure for organizing items
generator.generateTagsDescription: Create tags for flexible item labeling

ai.title: Create with AI
ai.subtitle: Describe your directory idea in natural language and let AI handle the setup
ai.formSubtitle: Describe your directory idea and let AI handle the setup and initial content generation

ai.promptPlaceholder: Describe what kind of directory you want to create, what items it should contain, and any specific requirements...
ai.inspirationText: Need inspiration? Try one of these:

0.name: AI Developer Tools
0.prompt: Create a directory of the best AI tools for developers

1.name: React Components Hub
1.prompt: Build a curated list of open-source React components

2.name: Remote Team Tools
2.prompt: Generate a directory of productivity apps for remote teams

3.name: Sustainable Fashion
3.prompt: Make a collection of sustainable fashion brands

ai.featuresTitle: What AI will do for you:

features.0: Generate an appropriate name and description
features.2: Find and add relevant items automatically
features.3: Set up GitHub repository with proper documentation

ai.noteText: AI generation typically takes 2-5 minutes depending on the complexity of your request. You'll be notified when your directory is ready.

research.title: Import & Research Mode
research.description: Items from the source repository will be used as research seeds. The AI pipeline will discover new items, rewrite descriptions, and expand the taxonomy.

research.enrichDescriptionsHint: AI will rewrite and expand all imported descriptions
research.expandTaxonomyHint: Generate new categories and tags beyond the source taxonomy
research.parsePrIssuesHint: Extract additional items from pull requests and issues
research.legalDescription: Research mode uses the source repository as input data only. The final collection will consist primarily of newly discovered items with original, AI-generated descriptions. Source content is not copied verbatim.
